task_completion.description: The agent identifies and fixes the first bug in the src/ directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the agent editing Python files in /home/ga/workspace/pycache/src/ to correct a bug, OR terminal output of pytest showing at least 1 test passing (or a reduction in the number of failing tests).

task_completion.description: The agent identifies and fixes a second bug in the src/ directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a second distinct code correction in the src/ directory, OR terminal output of pytest showing at least 2 tests passing.

task_completion.description: The agent identifies and fixes a third bug in the src/ directory, reaching the pass threshold.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a third distinct code correction in the src/ directory, OR terminal output of pytest showing at least 3 tests passing.

task_completion.description: The agent identifies and fixes a fourth bug in the src/ directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a fourth distinct code correction in the src/ directory, OR terminal output of pytest showing at least 4 tests passing.

task_completion.description: The agent identifies and fixes the fifth bug in the src/ directory and verifies all fixes by running pytest.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the final code correction in the src/ directory AND a terminal running 'pytest' with output indicating all tests have passed successfully (e.g., 100% passing, no failures).

integrity.description: The agent genuinely edited the source code files rather than mocking the test output.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a text editor or IDE (like VS Code, vim, or nano) with modified code in the src/ directory, not just terminal commands echoing a fake 'passed' message.

integrity.description: The agent did not modify the test files to force them to pass.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should only be seen editing files within the 'src/' directory as instructed. There should be no evidence of the agent altering or deleting files in the tests directory.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely ran the pytest command to verify the fixes.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should show the actual execution of 'pytest' (or 'python -m pytest') with standard pytest output formatting (e.g., collected items, progress percentages, green text for passes).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires fixing exactly 5 bugs in the src/ directory. Each fixed bug is worth 20 points. A minimum of 60 points (3 fixed bugs) is required to pass. The VLM should rely on pytest output to confirm the number of successful fixes.
